key_highlights: Global tech sell-off intensifies, led by AI and chip stocks, with South Korea leading the rout and Micron's blowout outlook providing a late-day boost.
Oil prices fall below $75 for the first time since March as Hormuz traffic recovers, flooding markets with supply and erasing wartime gains.
Gold breaks below $4,000 as multi-year rally grinds to a halt, while silver also slips, struggling more than gold.
Bitcoin breaks below $60,000 again as biggest crypto buyers balk, with rate hike fears weighing on prices.
House passes sweeping bill to lower housing costs, sending it to Trump's desk, while Americans warm up to homebuying for the first time in 3 years.
Big banks pass Fed stress test, paving way for payouts, and Fed's Bowman finalizes moves to reshape bank-supervision unit.
Trump administration announces $17.5 billion in loans for 10 new large nuclear reactors, signaling major energy policy shift.
SpaceX stock tumbles 23% from its high, then raises $25 billion in debt sale less than two weeks after IPO.
ECB's Zigman says price stability is key, cheaper oil will help, while Bank of Canada rejects recession label.
Alan Greenspan, economist and longtime head of the Federal Reserve, dies at 100.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off with selective strength. Global tech rout intensifies, led by AI and chip stocks, but Micron's blowout outlook (revenue quadrupling, stock up 10%) provides a counter-narrative. SK Hynix caps success with $29 billion US listing. Nvidia and SpaceX drive high-grade bond sales to a June record. AI bubble concerns persist with comparisons to 1999.
sectors.implications: High volatility and divergence between AI leaders and laggards. Opportunities in memory chips (Micron, SK Hynix) and AI infrastructure, but caution on overvalued names. CrowdStrike's stock split may spark next run. Ciena outperforms S&P 500.

sectors.trend: Oil falls below $75 as Hormuz traffic recovers, flooding markets with supply. Wartime gains erased. Shale bosses say Trump's Iran posts sow chaos. LNG exports could normalize within weeks. Qatar says exports could return to normal. Stonepeak raises $2.5B in private bonds tied to LNG plant.
sectors.implications: Bearish for oil prices short-term, benefiting consumers and airlines but hurting energy stocks. Midstream implications mixed. Nuclear renaissance with $17.5B loans for 10 new reactors.

sectors.trend: Gold breaks below $4,000, multi-year rally halts. Silver slips, struggling more than gold. Bitcoin breaks below $60,000 again as buyers balk. Rate hike fears weigh. Michael Saylor's MSTR should pause BTC buying. Grant Cardone buying 'like crazy'.
sectors.implications: Risk-off sentiment and rising real yields pressure non-yielding assets. Crypto faces headwinds from rate hikes and regulatory uncertainty. Gold may find support if recession fears grow.

sectors.trend: Big banks pass Fed stress test, paving way for payouts. Fed's Bowman reshapes bank-supervision unit. Basis trade key driver of hedge fund Treasury exposure. Benchmarks are broken for fixed income. Best CD rates up to 4% APY, money market up to 4.01% APY, high-yield savings up to 4.10% APY.
sectors.implications: Bank stocks may rally on stress test results and payout increases. Fixed income investors should seek active management given broken benchmarks. Rising rates benefit savers but pressure bond prices.

sectors.trend: House passes sweeping bill to lower housing costs, sending to Trump's desk. Americans warming up to homebuying for first time in 3 years. Mortgage rates mixed. HELOC and home equity loan rates discussed. Berkeley warns flat delivery times jump to eight years. Blackstone's $343M loan on Chicago tower defaults.
sectors.implications: Housing bill could boost affordability and demand. Homebuilder and REIT stocks may benefit. However, construction delays and commercial real estate stress (Blackstone default) remain risks.

sectors.trend: Summer sales events kick off as inflation squeezes U.S. consumers. Target to open 11 stores in July. Coke, Pepsi, Dr Pepper to use QR codes for ingredients. Amazon Prime Day deals found. EOFY sales in Australia. Casey's stock outperforms Dow.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending under pressure but discount events may drive traffic. Retailers with strong value propositions (Target, Casey's) may fare better. QR codes signal transparency trend.

sectors.trend: Dutch government irritated by U.S. plans for new ASML export restrictions. Major breach in Europe-US relationship. California takes on Trump in new legal battle. Trump proposal would jack up citizenship fees by up to 80%. UK tribunal gives go-ahead for $4B lawsuit against Apple over iCloud.
sectors.implications: Tech supply chain disruptions risk (ASML). Transatlantic tensions may impact trade and defense. Legal battles add uncertainty. Apple faces regulatory headwinds in UK.

key_themes: AI Bubble or Breakout? Tech sell-off vs. Micron/SK Hynix strength
Oil Supply Glut: Hormuz reopening crashes prices
Precious Metals & Crypto Correction: Gold below $4K, Bitcoin below $60K
Rate Hike Fears: ECB, Fed, Bank of Canada hawkishness
Housing Policy Shift: Bill to lower costs, improving sentiment
Banking Resilience: Stress test pass, payout expectations
SpaceX Volatility: IPO to debt sale, stock swings
Geopolitical Fractures: US-Europe, US-China tech tensions
Consumer Squeeze: Inflation, summer sales, discounting

risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify into value and defensive sectors; reduce exposure to high-multiple AI names; consider hedges via put options on QQQ or SMH.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil Price Collapse & Energy Sector Stress
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce overweight in energy equities; benefit from lower input costs via consumer/transport stocks; monitor midstream MLPs for yield stability.
Reduce gold and crypto allocations; consider short-term treasuries or TIPS for inflation hedge; wait for clearer rate path before re-entering.
Diversify geographically; favor domestic over export-oriented tech; monitor ASML and semiconductor export restrictions.
Avoid CRE-heavy REITs and regional banks; favor residential and industrial real estate; monitor Blackstone and other large landlords.
Lock in fixed income yields now (CDs, money market); favor floating-rate bonds; reduce duration in bond portfolios.

opportunities.rationale: Blowout outlook with revenue quadrupling, memory crunch driving prices higher. Strong AI demand tailwind. Stock jumped 10% but still has upside.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate SK Hynix (HXSCL) on US listing
opportunities.rationale: Come-from-behind AI success, $29 billion US listing seizes on AI demand. Memory chip leader with strong fundamentals.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in nuclear energy ETFs (e.g., NLR, URA)
opportunities.rationale: $17.5B in loans for 10 new large reactors signals major policy shift. Nuclear renaissance benefits uranium miners and reactor builders.
Housing bill to lower costs, Americans warming to homebuying. Mortgage rates mixed but sentiment improving. Target store openings also positive.

opportunities.recommendation: Lock in high-yield savings and CDs at 4%+ APY
opportunities.rationale: Rates remain elevated; locking in now provides risk-free return. Best money market accounts up to 4.01%, CDs up to 4%, high-yield savings up to 4.10%.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-multiple AI and tech stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Global tech sell-off intensifying, AI bubble comparisons to 1999. Rotate to value and defensive sectors.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil price exposure via short energy ETFs or long consumer stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil below $75, Hormuz reopening floods supply. Energy sector under pressure. Benefit from lower input costs via airlines, transport, consumer discretionary.
Gold breaks below $4,000, Bitcoin below $60,000. Rate hike fears weigh. Move to cash or short-duration bonds.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to bank stocks on stress test results
defensive_moves.rationale: Big banks pass Fed stress test, paving way for payouts. Bank stocks may rally. Fed's Bowman reshaping supervision could be positive.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid commercial real estate and regional banks
defensive_moves.rationale: Blackstone $343M loan default, Berkeley warns of 8-year delays. CRE stress persists. Regional banks exposed.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Cautious with downside risks. Tech sell-off may continue as AI bubble fears persist, but Micron's strength could stabilize semis. Oil likely to remain under $80 as supply floods. Gold and crypto may test lower supports. Housing bill could provide a floor for homebuilder stocks. Rate hike fears may ease if oil deflation feeds into lower inflation. Focus on earnings season for AI capex confirmation.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Moderately positive. Nuclear renaissance and housing policy shift are structural positives. AI infrastructure spending (Nvidia, SpaceX bonds) suggests long-term demand. Oil supply normalization benefits global economy. Rate cuts possible if inflation falls. Geopolitical risks (US-Europe, US-China) need monitoring. Fixed income yields attractive for locking in.
key_catalysts: Fed rate decision and dot plot in July
ECB and Bank of Canada policy moves
AI earnings season (Nvidia, AMD, Micron, SK Hynix)
SpaceX stock performance and debt market access
watch_list: WTI crude oil price action around $70 support
